title: Prenatal Exposure to Select Phthalates and Phenols and Associations with Fetal and Placental Weight among Male Births in the EDEN Cohort (France).
ehr_evidence: The study uses the EDEN cohort, which involves spot urine samples collected during gestation, not EHR.
summary: A prospective cohort study investigating the associations between maternal prenatal exposure to phthalates and phenols and placental weight and placental-to-birth weight ratio (PFR) in male births in France.
key_findings: Triclosan and MCNP were negatively associated with placental weight
Benzophenone-3 and the sum of parabens were positively associated with placental weight
MCNP and MCOP were negatively associated with the placental-to-birth weight ratio (PFR)
population: 473 mother-son pairs in the EDEN cohort (France)
